2605.03058.title: Neuron-Anchored Rule Extraction for Large Language Models via Contrastive Hierarchical Ablation
2605.03058.paper_summary: A key goal of explainable AI (XAI) is to express the decision logic of large language models (LLMs) in symbolic form and link it to internal mechanisms. Global rule-extraction methods typically learn symbolic surrogates without grounding rules in model circuitry, while mechanistic interpretability can connect behaviors to neuron sets but often depends on hand-crafted hypotheses and expensive neuron-level interventions. We introduce MechaRule, a pipeline that grounds rule extraction in LLM circuits by efficiently localizing sparse neurons called agonists, whose activation neutralization disrupts rule-related behaviors. MechaRule rests on two empirical observations. First, within a fixed baseline/flip regime, sparse agonist effects can be approximately monotone and saturating: a few dominant neuron activations can overtop weaker ones at coarse scales, while overlapping neurons flip many of the same examples. This motivates viewing localization as adaptive group testing driven by a regime-conditional strength predicate with confidence-guided conservative pruning, yielding Theta(k log(N/k) + k) interventions over N candidates when k << N neurons are agonists under the monotone-overtopping abstraction. Second, agonists emerge more reliably when ablations are verified through data splits aligned with close-to-faithful rule behavior; spectral splits remain a useful rule-free fallback, while unfaithful splits degrade localization. Empirically, overtopping appears mainly in learned, task-aligned regimes: on arithmetic and jailbreak tasks across Qwen2 and GPT-J, MechaRule recalls 96.8% of high-effect brute-force agonists in completed comparisons, and suppressing localized agonists reduces arithmetic accuracy and jailbreak success by up to 71.1% and 8.8%, respectively.
2605.03058.paper_summary_zh: 一個可解釋人工智慧 (XAI) 的關鍵目標是以符號形式表達大型語言模型 (LLMs) 的決策邏輯，並將其與內部機制連結。全球規則提取方法通常學習符號替代品，而不將規則與模型電路基礎相連接，而機械解釋性可以將行為與神經元集合連結，但通常依賴於手工製作的假設和昂貴的神經元級干預。我們介紹了 MechaRule，一個將規則提取基於 LLM 電路的流程，通過有效定位稱為激動劑的稀疏神經元，這些神經元的激活中和會破壞與規則相關的行為。MechaRule 基於兩個實證觀察。首先，在固定的基線/翻轉範疇內，稀疏激動劑的效應可以近似單調且飽和：少數主導神經元的激活可以在粗略尺度上超越較弱的激活，而重疊的神經元翻轉許多相同的例子。這促使我們將定位視為由條件強度謂詞驅動的自適應群體測試，並進行信心引導的保守修剪，當 k << N 神經元是單調超越抽象下的激動劑時，對 N 個候選者進行 Theta(k log(N/k) + k) 次干預。其次，當通過與接近忠實規則行為對齊的數據拆分驗證切除時，激動劑的出現更為可靠；頻譜拆分仍然是一個有用的無規則備用方案，而不忠實的拆分會降低定位效果。實證上，超越主要出現在學習的、與任務對齊的範疇內：在 Qwen2 和 GPT-J 的算術和越獄任務中，MechaRule 在完成的比較中回憶起 96.8% 的高效能暴力激動劑，並且抑制定位的激動劑分別使算術準確度和越獄成功率降低了 71.1% 和 8.8%。
2605.03058.author: Francesco Sovrano et.al.
2605.03058.authors: Francesco Sovrano, Gabriele Dominici, Marc Langheinrich
